policy.storage: Link-only. Do not download, scrape, vendor, train on, or package third-party photos, footage, captions, or comments until explicit rights are recorded per item.
policy.shipping_rule: First-party capture, official/public-domain media with item metadata, or explicit written permission is required before a reference can become a production asset.
policy.social_rule: Public social links and hashtag/search pages are fidelity-review seeds only. Record platform, creator, date, observed flow context, and permission status before any asset use.
policy.guide_review: Guide/outfitter notes can validate lines, hazards, surf/flush/pin behavior, rescue visibility, and seasonal flow appearance, but guidebook text and third-party captions remain link-only unless rights clear.

reference_goals: Chili Bar put-in, Troublemaker/Meatgrinder-style boulder gardens, oak/conifer riparian banks, exposed late-summer rocks, commercial summer flow color, high-flow foam lines
Guide-seat downstream framing, swimmer visibility, eddy/recovery pools, and bank vegetation density

candidate_links.notes: Use as official context and a starting point for public-domain/attribution review; do not assume every embedded image is shippable without item metadata.
Review each file page, license, author, date, and attribution before using as more than reference.
Use search results only as leads; do not download images without license/permission review.
Record only public link, creator handle, date, and observed conditions after manual review; never vendor the media itself.

reference_goals: Lees Ferry launch, Grand Canyon canyon-wall scale, sand bars, big-water laterals, boils, wave trains, oar-rig sightlines, dam-release water color
Sparse desert riparian vegetation, long swimmer/rescue sightlines, river-mile context, canyon atmospheric depth

candidate_links.notes: NPS media is the preferred first review path for Colorado visual reference, but each image/video still needs item metadata and credit review.
Use for contextual validation; do not treat embedded media as assets until item rights are recorded.
Review each file page and avoid guidebook/caption reuse unless rights explicitly allow it.
Use only for manual fidelity notes and permission outreach; no media import without rights.

reference_goals: Rainforest gorge density, steep green banks, clear-season versus rainy-season water color, waterfalls, hanging vegetation, tropical humidity and mist
Commercial raft scale, rescue visibility under canopy, bank vegetation pressure, post-rain pushy wave trains

candidate_links.notes: Treat as candidate references only until each file page, author, license, and attribution are recorded.
Use search results only as leads; do not download images without license/permission review.
Record only public link, creator handle, date, and observed flow/weather notes after manual review.
Use as manual review leads for motion and line choices; no footage extraction or audio use without explicit rights.

next_actions: Manually review candidate pages and promote only item-level references with recorded author, license, date, station/reach, observed flow or rainfall context, and permission status.
Attach first-party guide/outfitter review notes for each promoted reference.
Use the references to drive biome-specific asset selection and environment art reviews, not as downloaded source assets.
